Methodology

Data Overview

The raw dataset was provided as part of the VAST Challenge 2025 Mini-Challenge 1 and consists of a knowledge graph representing the music industry, delivered as a JSON file and then split into two CSV files:

File Records Description
mc1_nodes.csv 17,412 All entities in the graph — Person (11,361), Song (3,615), RecordLabel (1,217), Album (996), MusicalGroup (223)
mc1_edges.csv 37,857 All relationships — PerformerOf, ComposerOf, LyricistOf, ProducerOf, RecordedBy, DistributedBy, InStyleOf, InterpolatesFrom, CoverOf, DirectlySamples, LyricalReferenceTo, MemberOf

Data Wrangling

The graph-structured data (nodes + edges) needed to be reshaped into flat tables for Tableau to consume. A Python script (wrangle.py) was written using only the standard library (csv and collections modules) to perform this transformation.

No data was deleted, modified, imputed, or fabricated. All values in the output tables originate directly from the source files.

Operations Performed

The wrangling consisted of five standard operations:

Operation Description
Filter Select rows matching criteria (e.g., genre = “Oceanus Folk”, Sailor Shift’s edges, influence-type edges)
Join / Lookup Match edge source/target IDs to node attributes like name, genre, and release year — equivalent to a SQL JOIN
Aggregate / Count Count occurrences such as number of songs per artist, influence references per song, and collaborators per work
Reshape / Flatten Convert the graph structure into flat tabular rows (e.g., one row per influence relationship instead of separate node and edge records)
Derive Compute new fields from existing data (e.g., Career_Span = Last_Release − First_Release, Is_Oceanus_Folk flag)

Key Entities Identified

Through filtering, the following key entities were identified for analysis:

  • Sailor Shift (Person, id=17255) — the central figure of the investigation
  • Sailor Shift’s works — 38 works (17 Songs + 21 Albums) linked via PerformerOf, ComposerOf, LyricistOf, and ProducerOf edges
  • Ivy Echos (MusicalGroup, id=17260) — Sailor Shift’s band, found via MemberOf edge
  • Oceanus Folk catalogue — 305 works filtered by genre = “Oceanus Folk”

Output Tables

The wrangling produced 6 Tableau-ready CSV files, each designed to answer specific questions from the VAST Challenge task:

Table 1: sailor_shift_career.csv (38 rows)

Sailor Shift’s full discography with release year, genre, collaborators, record label, and what influenced each work. Supports the career profile timeline visualisation.

Columns: Song, Type, Genre, Release_Year, Notable, Single, Record_Label, Performers, Composers, Lyricists, Num_Collaborators, Influenced_By, Num_Influences

Table 2: sailor_shift_influences.csv (33 rows)

All influence relationships to and from Sailor Shift — who influenced her (26 edges) and who she influenced (7 edges). Answers: “Who has she been most influenced by over time?”

Columns: Direction, Influence_Type, Sailor_Song, Sailor_Song_Year, Other_Song, Other_Song_Genre, Other_Song_Year, Other_Artist

Table 3: sailor_shift_collaborators.csv (77 rows)

Every collaborator on Sailor Shift’s works with their role. Answers: “Who has she collaborated with?”

Columns: Song, Release_Year, Genre, Collaborator, Role

Table 4: oceanus_folk_spread.csv (601 rows)

All influence flows in and out of the Oceanus Folk genre. Tracks how the genre’s influence spread over time and which genres were most affected. Answers: “How has the influence of Oceanus Folk spread through the musical world?”

Columns: Direction, Influence_Type, OF_Song, OF_Song_Year, OF_Artist, Other_Song, Other_Genre, Other_Year, Other_Artist

Table 5: oceanus_folk_timeline.csv (305 rows)

All Oceanus Folk songs with metadata for building the genre’s growth timeline. Flags Sailor Shift’s contributions.

Columns: Song, Release_Year, Notable, Single, Artist, Record_Label, Draws_From_Count, Influenced_Others_Count, Is_Sailor_Shift

Table 6: artist_careers.csv (872 rows)

Career profiles for all artists/groups with 3+ songs. Includes song count, genre, career span, influence score, and collaborator count. Supports rising star comparison and prediction tasks.

Columns: Artist, Node_Type, Num_Songs, Primary_Genre, All_Genres, First_Release, Last_Release, Career_Span, Notable_Songs, Influence_Score, Num_Collaborators, Is_Oceanus_Folk

Tools Used

Tool Purpose
Python 3 (standard library only) Data wrangling — reshaping graph data into flat tables
Gephi Network exploration — discovering patterns, communities, and key entities
